label: Scandinavian
prompt: Phase One IQ4 or Canon EOS R5 with 24mm tilt‑shift lens, f/5.6, ISO 100, tripod, perfect verticals — eye‑level perspective, standing at the room entrance, slightly off‑center to the right to reveal depth. Photorealistic 8K, magazine cover quality, rich tactile detail. Scene: ultra‑luxurious Scandinavian living room in a $10M home — light oak wide‑plank floors, crisp matte white plaster walls, floor‑to‑ceiling glazing flooding GOLDEN HOUR light. ONE clear hero: a Muuto Outline sofa in muted dusty rose centered-left, layered with cashmere and linen cushions in sage and powder blue, a chunky hand‑knit wool throw and a long white Danish sheepskin draped over the arm. Foreground left: Hay About A Lounge chair in natural oak with brass legs, draped sheepskin; midground: Muuto Around low coffee table in oiled oak with a sculptural cluster of lit pillar candles, matte glazed ceramics, and a hand‑carved wooden tray — candles cast warm amber highlights and soft shadows as the sun pours through. Background right: bespoke light‑oak built‑in shelving with curated ceramics (Kähler‑style), linen books, and a single brass object; left: honed Carrara marble hearth with a subtle recessed fireplace. Accessories: Menu/JWDA floor lamp with brushed brass, hand‑thrown vases, a thick natural wool rug (muted oatmeal), tactile surfaces — open‑pored oak, soft velvet, chunky knit, matte ceramics, satin brass. Color palette: warm whites, light oak, muted pastels, amber highlights — rich, warm tones with strong value contrast (bright sunlit highlights vs deep window recess shadows). Composition: clean framing, one strong focal point (sofa + lit candles) with bold Scandinavian markers (Hay + Muuto, sheepskin, knit textures, light oak) — instantly recognizable even at thumbnail size. Warm, inviting, aspirational, tactile, magazine‑cover luxurious.

label: Modern
prompt:
Phase One IQ4 or Canon EOS R5 with 24mm tilt-shift, f/5.6, ISO 100, tripod, perfect verticals. Eye-level, slightly off-center perspective showing depth as if standing in the room. Photorealistic 8K, magazine-cover quality. Golden hour: warm natural sunlight spilling through floor-to-ceiling glass, strong rim-light on surfaces, long soft shadows; complemented by warm ambient interior lighting (dimmed brass sconces, amber glass pendant).

Hero composition: low-profile Minotti “Hamilton” sofa in warm ivory leather, centered slightly off axis, sun rim-lit to create a single clear focal point with high contrast against dark floor. Dark wide-plank oak floors, hand-oiled, deep chocolate tone; clean geometric white walls with deep rectilinear reveals evocative of a Richard Meier penthouse. Sculptural B&B Italia boucle armchair in caramel, cognac leather ottoman, and a single sculptural plaster art object on a limestone plinth for strong recognizable shapes.

Coffee table: polished black Calacatta marble with bold white veins and a slim brushed brass base, styled with a small stack of art books and a single amber glass vase. Statement lighting: large Bocci 14 cluster in warm amber glass suspended low above the table, creating bright highlights and jewel-toned reflections. Floor-to-ceiling glass with ultra-thin matte black frames, distant skyline glowing in golden hour backlight.

Textures tactile and visible: wool-loop sand rug, hand-stitched leather seams, boucle throws, brass hardware, honed marble hearth. Rich, warm color palette—ivory, caramel, cognac, deep chocolate, warm brass—no flat or washed-out colors. Strong value contrast, bold geometric lines, one unmistakable hero object, clean framing, aspirational and inviting—looks and reads instantly as an ultra-luxurious $10M penthouse interior cover shot.

label: Luxury
prompt:
Camera: Phase One IQ4 or Canon EOS R5, 24mm tilt‑shift, f/5.6, ISO 100, tripod, perfect verticals. Eye‑level perspective, slightly off‑center, showing depth toward floor‑to‑ceiling panoramic city views. Single, clear hero composition: a deep emerald velvet Fendi Casa low‑profile sofa centered beneath a large crystal chandelier, facing a brushed‑gold coffee table and a Versace Home cream leather accent chair — focal point immediately readable in a thumbnail.

Scene: double‑height living room in a restrained ultra‑luxury penthouse that reads like a tasteful $10M home. Polished Calacatta Oro marble floors with warm gold veining, wide planks of dark walnut paneling, hand‑stitched silk throw pillows in honey and tobacco tones, sculpted brushed‑brass side tables, and a black lacquer console with gold inlay. Crystal chandelier with large faceted drops casts warm specular highlights. Minimal, elegant accessories: a single large ceramic vase, art book stack, and a plush cashmere throw folded precisely.

Lighting: golden hour natural light flooding through glazing, warm rim light outlining the sofa and metallics, long soft shadows across marble. Complement with warm ambient cove lighting and a dimmed gold‑tinted floor lamp to lift shadow detail. Emphasize tactile materials: velvet nap, silk sheen, cold polished marble, warm brushed brass micro‑scratches, crystal facets. High dynamic range, photorealistic, 8K, magazine cover quality — rich, warm color palette (emerald, honey gold, tobacco, cream), strong value contrast, deep shadows and bright highlights.

Composition notes for clarity at thumbnail size: bold, recognizable markers (emerald velvet, crystal chandelier, marble floor, gold accents), clean framing, single focal plane, crisp textures, and a palpable aspirational, inviting mood.

label: Minimal
prompt:
Phase One IQ4 (or Canon EOS R5 w/ 24mm tilt‑shift), f/5.6, ISO 100, tripod, perfect verticals. Eye‑level, slightly off‑center perspective showing depth — like you are standing in the room. Photorealistic, 8K, magazine cover quality.

Minimal living room in the spirit of John Pawson meets Axel Vervoordt: white hand‑troweled plaster walls with subtle undulation, seamless built‑ins in the same plaster, vast negative space. One statement sofa: low‑profile, single long seat in warm flax linen (deep honey‑beige / ochre undertone), raw‑edge seams, generous cushions with visible natural slubbing — tactile, inviting. Floor: wide‑plank reclaimed oak, warm honey patina, strong grain and hand‑worn texture. Single sculptural ceramic: large matte thrown vessel in soft umber with visible throwing marks and a slightly irregular lip, placed alone on a short, square honed limestone plinth near the sofa — the only object in the room.

Lighting: golden hour sunlight streaming through floor‑to‑ceiling sheer ivory curtains from the left, warm directional shafts creating long, soft shadows and rim light on linen fibers; complemented by concealed warm cove lighting in the recesses to enhance depth and material warmth. Strong value contrast: bright plaster bathed in gold, deep oak floor shadow, rich linen midtones, dark umber ceramic focal point.

Composition for thumbnail readability: one clear hero — the sofa and ceramic plinth — bold, instantly recognizable silhouette against a luminous plaster backdrop, clean framing, negative space defining form. Rich, warm color palette, tactile materials (plaster, slub linen, oak, honed stone, matte ceramic), serene monastic calm yet unmistakably luxurious — feels like a $10M home.

label: Industrial
prompt:
Phase One IQ4 or Canon EOS R5 with 24mm tilt-shift, f/5.6, ISO 100, tripod. Perfect verticals. Eye-level, slightly off-center perspective — feel like you're standing in a converted SoHo warehouse loft. HERO: worn cognac leather Chesterfield sofa (deep buttoned, patinated grain) placed slightly left-of-center on a large hand-knotted Persian rug (deep burgundy, navy, gold accents) — strong focal point with high contrast between warm leather and saturated rug. Backlit by massive steel-framed industrial windows pouring GOLDEN HOUR sunlight across the space, creating long warm highlights and soft painterly shadows. Polished concrete floor with gentle reflective sheen, subtle wet-look reflections of the sofa and pendant lights.

Industrial architectural markers pronounced: exposed red-brown brick wall with crisp mortar joints, blackened steel I-beams, raw metal shelving (cold-rolled steel, visible welds) filled with curated objects — leather-bound books, aged brass vases, terracotta pots, vintage camera, folded wool throws. Vintage factory pendant lights and warm Edison bulbs hang low over a solid walnut and blackened steel coffee table with honed Carrara marble inset — bulbs glowing amber, mixing with sunlight for rich ambers and copper highlights. Add tall fiddle-leaf fig in a textured cement planter and a worn leather lounge chair with brass nailhead trim to balance composition.

Materials tactile and hyperreal: distressed leather texture, rough brick, brushed steel, honed marble veining, soft wool weave visible at thumbnail size. Strong leading lines from shelving and beams guide eye toward windows; bold value contrast between sunlit window plane and deep shadowed corners ensures instant readability as a small thumbnail. Photorealistic, 8K, magazine cover quality, luxurious, warm, lived-in, aspirational.

label: Bohemian
prompt: Shot with Phase One IQ4 (or Canon EOS R5) using a 24mm tilt‑shift, f/5.6, ISO 100, tripod — perfect verticals, eye‑level perspective slightly off‑center to show depth as if standing in the room. Photorealistic, 8K, magazine‑cover quality. Hero composition: oversized rattan peacock chair (natural cane) placed slightly right of center, backlit by golden‑hour sunset pouring through tall arched windows; rim light crisply defines cane weave. Layered kilim rugs (hand‑knotted Persian/Moroccan patterns, rich red, burnt sienna, indigo) cascade across aged wide‑plank oak floors. Low solid walnut coffee table, live edge, covered with stacked art monographs (Taschen), sculptural beeswax candles, a Moroccan brass tray with a vintage amber glass decanter. Large macramé wall hanging in creamy cotton dominates left wall — visible knot texture, heavy bohemian scale. Embroidered silk and velvet cushions (emerald, ruby, sapphire) and a sheepskin throw draped over the chair for tactile contrast. Hanging plants everywhere: large monstera, trailing pothos and string‑of‑hearts in terracotta and glazed ceramic pots — lush greenery silhouetted by warm light. Hand‑plastered terracotta walls with subtle limewash texture, brass wall sconce with warm filament bulb for ambient glow. Small limestone fireplace hearth with a few curated objects (aged leather pouf, carved wooden bowl). Strong value contrast: sunlit warm terracotta, deep shadowed jewel tones, bright highlights on brass and glass. Composition is bold and instantly readable at thumbnail size — the peacock chair + macramé + arched window silhouette create an unmistakable bohemian statement. Emphasize rich materiality: visible weave, knot work, pile of rugs, patina on brass. Mood: free‑spirited, maximalist, curated luxury — feels like a $10M home, tactile, warm, inviting.

label: Japandi
prompt: Photorealistic 8K magazine-cover interior photograph, shot with Phase One IQ4 or Canon EOS R5 + 24mm tilt-shift lens, f/5.6, ISO 100, tripod, perfect verticals. Eye‑level, slightly off‑center perspective that reads like you are standing in the room, strong depth. Golden hour warm natural light flooding through a large backlit shōji screen, creating a soft luminous grid of highlights and long, warm shadows; complemented by subtle warm ambient interior lighting (low brass floor lamp with frosted glass). ONE clear hero composition: low-profile solid quarter-sawn oak bench/sofa with visible hand-sanded grain and precision joinery placed slightly right of center; in the immediate foreground, a sculptural hand-thrown matte black ceramic vase on a low oak coffee table holds an elegant Ikebana branch — this vase and branch are the sharp focal point with strong value contrast against the warm shōji glow. Tatami‑inspired woven wool-rush rug with straw texture and charcoal wool selvedge leads the eye inward. Charcoal linen cushions, silk lumbar pillow in warm cream, a single folded hemp throw. Background: translucent shōji panels, a floating oak media shelf with matte ceramic vessels and a small handcrafted brass inlay tray. Materials tactile and luxurious — open-pore oak, nubby wool, soft silk sheen, raw porcelain texture, hand-polished brass. Color palette: warm whites, deep charcoal, honey oak, soft beige — rich, saturated, warm tones, high value contrast for thumbnail readability. Minimal, contemplative styling with deliberate negative space and balanced asymmetry; wabi-sabi imperfection in handmade ceramics and slight wood knots for authenticity. Aim: aspirational, serene, perfectly balanced, feels like a $10M Japandi living room — immediately recognizable even at small sizes.

label: Mid-Century
prompt: Phase One IQ4 or Canon EOS R5 with 24mm tilt‑shift, f/5.6, ISO 100, tripod, perfect verticals, eye‑level perspective slightly off‑center as if standing in the room — show strong depth. Photorealistic 8K, magazine cover quality, ultra‑luxury mid‑century modern living room: foreground hero is an iconic Eames lounge chair in rich top‑grain mustard leather with visible creasing and polished rose brass pivots, angled slightly toward camera and bathed in a golden hour sunbeam that creates warm specular highlights and tactile shadows. Midground: classic Noguchi coffee table with clear glass top and warm walnut base, styling with a burnt orange glazed ceramic vase and a stack of leather‑bound design books. Background: long walnut credenza with sunburst clock above, hand‑finished walnut veneer, tapered legs visible, brass hardware catching light. Left: low mid‑century sofa in teal wool with a nubby mustard linen throw and burnt orange velvet cushion — all tapered legs. Right: brass arc floor lamp with warm filament bulb, terrazzo planter with large fiddle‑leaf fig (visible glossy leaves), honed terrazzo chips echo colors. Floors: warm walnut plank flooring, plush off‑white boucle rug anchoring seating. Walls: soft warm cream plaster with subtle texture. Lighting: dominant golden hour natural light flooding through tall windows, creating deep warm shadows, rim light on chair and leaves, complemented by warm ambient interior lamps to fill midtones. Materials hyper‑textural: leather grain, walnut veneer, polished brass, glass, boucle, terrazzo clearly readable. Composition: one bold focal point (Eames lounge), strong value contrast, saturated mustard/teal/burnt orange palette, clean framing, immediately recognizable mid‑century silhouettes and tapered legs — aspirational, inviting, “1960s Palm Springs meets Architectural Digest,” looks like a $10M home, luxurious, tactile, intimate.

label: Art Deco
prompt: Phase One IQ4 (or Canon EOS R5) with 24mm tilt-shift lens, f/5.6, ISO 100, tripod, perfect verticals, eye-level slightly off-center perspective showing depth — photorealistic 8K, magazine-cover quality. HERO: a curving emerald velvet sofa with deep channel tufting and brass plinth, placed front-left, catching a warm golden-hour beam across its hand-stitched pile; opposite, a low navy velvet club chair with brass piping. Center-right focal anchor: oversized Art Deco statement chandelier — layered bronze arms, fan-shaped brass motifs and cascading crystal drops — backlit by floor-to-ceiling arched windows with bronze mullions. Floor: dramatic chevron white and nero statuario marble, high polish, strong leading lines pulling the eye into the room. Back wall: lacquered black mirrored console with fan-shaped brass inlay and a large sunburst brass mirror, reflecting chandelier sparkle. Styling details: lacquered ebonized cocktail table with mother-of-pearl inlay, a pair of stacked brass and glass side tables, geometric silk toss pillows in emerald/navy/gold, a navy silk area rug with subtle gold filigree. Materials tactile and visible: burnished brass, hand-polished lacquer gloss, velvet nap, honed marble veins, cut-crystal facets, brushed bronze window frames. Lighting: golden-hour sunlight pouring through windows creating bold highlights and soft shadows, augmented by warm amber wall sconces and table lamps to enrich shadows and midtones. Composition for thumbnail readability: one clear hero (emerald sofa + chandelier silhouette) with high value contrast and saturated warm tones — emerald, deep navy, molten gold — bold geometric patterns and fan motifs instantly readable at small sizes. Mood: The Great Gatsby–level glamour, theatrical maximal elegance, extremely luxurious and inviting, every surface tactile and sumptuous.

label: Coastal
prompt:
Phase One IQ4 or Canon EOS R5 with 24mm tilt‑shift, f/5.6, ISO 100, tripod, perfect verticals. Eye‑level, slightly off‑center 3/4 view toward floor‑to‑ceiling windows revealing ocean — stand in the room, show depth. Photorealistic, 8K, magazine cover quality.

Scene: coastal luxury living room with crisp white shiplap walls and wide, bleached light‑oak plank floors (visible grain, hand‑sanded finish). Hero: a bespoke 3‑seat slipcovered sofa in slubby Belgian flax linen (warm white) placed center‑right, slightly angled toward the window. Accent: one deep navy velvet lumbar pillow and two soft blue washed‑cotton throw pillows for a bold pop and thumbnail readability. Front center: a sculptural hand‑carved driftwood coffee table (raw texture, weathered grey‑sand tones) with a low matte white ceramic bowl of bleached shells, a stack of large coffee‑table books, and a folded sand‑colored cashmere throw.

Accents: woven rattan occasional chair and rattan basket, natural jute area rug with visible weave, large potted Kentia palm left foreground whose fronds catch golden hour light and create dynamic silhouettes. Side table: small round bleached oak side table with an aged brass nautical lamp (warm patina, visible screws and rope detail) emitting warm ambient fill light. Styling details: blown glass decanter, sea‑glass vase with dried sea oats, polished shell sculpture.

Lighting: golden hour sunlight flooding through windows, warm rim highlights on linen and oak, long soft shadows across the floor, gentle backlight through palm fronds; complemented by warm interior lamp glow to lift midtones. Rich, warm palette — crisp whites, soft coastal blues, warm sand and honeyed brass — high value contrast: bright window & white shiplap vs. textured sofa, navy pillow, and dark driftwood. Composition clarity: single clear focal point (sofa + navy pillow + driftwood table) framed by palm and window, bold, instantly recognizable Malibu coastal luxury.

label: Rustic
prompt:
Phase One IQ4 or Canon EOS R5 with 24mm tilt‑shift lens, f/5.6, ISO 100, tripod, perfect verticals. Eye‑level, slightly off‑center perspective showing depth — stand in the room as if you might walk up to the hearth. Vertical/portrait crop for thumbnail clarity. Golden hour: warm amber sunlight floods through tall mullioned windows at right, casting dramatic shafts and specular highlights across the floor and seating; complemented by warm interior ambient lighting (antler chandelier, vintage brass lanterns, candlelight on the mantle) to lift shadows and create a glowing, enveloping warmth.

Hero composition: monumental honed bluestone fireplace as the centerpiece placed on the left third, flanked by built‑in stacked firewood cubbies. In front, two oversized low‑slung saddle‑brown leather club chairs (deep tufting, natural pull‑up patina) angled toward the hearth; one with a chunky hand‑knit cream wool throw draped over the arm — that throw is a foreground leading element for thumbnail readability. Antler chandelier (natural shed antlers with aged brass fittings) hangs slightly off center above, a bold style marker. Reclaimed wide‑plank oak floors, hand‑hewn exposed timber beams with visible tool marks, cowhide rug layered with a cream sheepskin. Vintage brass side table with hammered finish, sculptural bronze object and brass candlesticks on the stone mantle, hammered copper fireplace tools.

Photorealistic, 8K magazine cover quality — tactile materials, visible leather pores, wool fibers, wood grain, stone veining, brass patina. High contrast between warm sunlit highlights and deep, rich shadows for instant legibility at small scales. Mood: rustic luxury — Aspen lodge elegance: rugged, expensive, warm, aspirational, and immediately recognizable even as a tiny thumbnail.
